response:
To calculate the area of the hexagonal community center, we start by recognizing that a regular hexagon can be divided into six equilateral triangles. Each triangle has a side length of 30 meters. The area of one equilateral triangle is given by the formula:

$$
\text{Area} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times \text{side}^2
$$

Substituting the side length of 30 meters:

$$
\text{Area of one triangle} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 30^2 = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \times 900 = 225\sqrt{3}
$$

Since the hexagon is composed of six such triangles, the total area of the hexagon is:

$$
\text{Total area} = 6 \times 225\sqrt{3} = 1350\sqrt{3} \text{ square meters}
$$

Next, we determine the radius of the circular garden. The garden is inscribed in the hexagon, meaning its circumference touches the midpoints of the hexagon's sides. The distance from the center of the hexagon to the midpoint of a side (also known as the apothem) is given by:

$$
\text{Apothem} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times \text{side length}
$$

Substituting the side length of 30 meters:

$$
\text{Apothem} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 30 = 15\sqrt{3}
$$

This apothem is also the radius of the circular garden. Therefore, the area of the circular garden is:

$$
\text{Area of garden} = \pi \times (15\sqrt{3})^2 = \pi \times 675 = 675\pi \text{ square meters}
$$

The hexagonal community center has an area of $1350\sqrt{3}$ square meters, and the circular garden has an area of $675\pi$ square meters.
